blocks.content: Amy was handed bread and coffee.
Then, as if watching a performance, applause erupted from all around.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Don't bring me this kind of stuff anymore since I won't need it from now on! Got it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Oh, yes.
Did this mean her anger had subsided?
Shirone, who thought he wouldn't have to see her again, sighed in relief.
But ultimately, Amy's words only caused greater misunderstanding among the students.

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: She accepted it! Amy accepted Shirone's feelings!

blocks.speaker: Student
blocks.content: She even said she doesn't need coffee anymore. Does that mean he can just come as he is?
Amy, as if unwilling to respond, entered the library.
Seriel quickly followed her, unable to hide her excitement.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Hey! What are you planning to do? If it's true, it's great, but did you really mean it that way?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ugh, I don't know! Think whatever you want. Don't you have anything better to do? I'm done with this once I graduate anyway. Let them play their childish games among themselves!
Shirone, descending the library stairs, felt so lighthearted he could almost fly.
Ten days of hardship. Honestly, he hadn't expected it to end so quickly.
From tomorrow onward, he could head straight to the library without wasting time.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yay! I'll have time to read one more book!
The male students, lost in such delusions, watched Shirone's joyful sprint with envy.
Amy closed her book and sighed.
She had never found studying enjoyable, but she loved the library.
Compared to mingling with people who gossiped about others' love lives or cared more about others than themselves, this place was far better.
Even as midnight approached, the library was still crowded with students.
They had no interest in who was dating whom. They were racehorses, sprinting madly toward their dreams.
Observing them made her realize that even getting lost in idle thoughts was a waste of time.
Yet, she remained the top-ranked student in Class Four, and the thought of being overtaken never crossed her mind.
Seriel, sitting beside her, turned her head.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Huh? Why are you closing the book? Are you leaving already?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yeah. I'm feeling a bit troubled today. I'll just rest properly and wake up an hour earlier tomorrow.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Really? Then let's go together.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No. You don't need to rest just because of me. See you tomorrow.

blocks.speaker: Seriel
blocks.content: Alright, then. Get some rest. Make sure to wake up early tomorrow.
Amy smiled as she packed her bag.
Fortress Seriel was the only friend she had made in six years at school.
Though as much of a chatterbox as any other girl her age, she knew when to get serious.
Her grades were also stable at sixth place in Class Four, ensuring she could safely enter the graduating class.
Amy liked Seriel, who enjoyed mature competition over shallow friendships.
After cheering her friend on, Amy left the reading room.
Except for the person on duty, everyone had left, leaving the hallway silent.
For students, the school was a place of dreams, but for the staff, it was just a workplace to earn money.
Amy stretched to relieve the day's fatigue as she descended the stairs.
Then, in the darkness of the stairwell, she noticed someone standing there.
Pale skin with dark circles under the eyes. Black hair covering the cheeks.
Recognizing the man's face illuminated by moonlight, Amy's expression twisted instantly.
It was Ardius Jake.
A noble of the second rank, his family head was the royal treasury executor.
Given the特殊性 of managing the royal finances, the family's influence was said to rival that of the first rank.
Jake was arrogant and prickly.
But the real reason Amy disliked him was that he was a student with little talent who didn't even bother to work hard.
Though they attended the same Class Four, Jake was far from the level required to enter the graduating class.
Moreover, rumors circulated that he was the leader of a research group called the Black Magicians.
In Amy's opinion, the Black Magicians' field of study was figuring out how to torment students more talented than themselves.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Amy, you finished early today. I was planning to wait until midnight.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Why were you waiting for me? Don't you have anything better to do?

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Don't be like that. I heard the rumors. You opened your heart to some nobody named Shirone, right?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Sighed, pressing her forehead. Was he out of his mind? Coming all the way to the library to spout such pathetic nonsense.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: I warmed it up for you. Someone like Amy deserves nothing less. Would you dirty your mouth with cheap coffee?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What are you doing?

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Exactly what it looks like. Courting you. You know well the power of my family. I'll eventually take over my father's role managing the royal treasury. Doesn't that make me the perfect match for you?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Sorry, but I don't engage in private chatter in the library. So, you're wrong about the place, the timing, and—oh, I forgot the most important part—the person. I dislike you. So let's both be careful to avoid this kind of thing in the future.
Amy sidestepped him and continued downstairs.
Jake blocked her path by pressing his hand against the wall.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: What do you think you're doing?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Was I too gentle? I have my family's dignity to uphold, and this won't do. Isn't it the same for you? Aren't you studying like mad to avoid tarnishing the Carmis family name? We share the same struggles.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: A money-grubbing miser like you.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: What did you say?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: My family lives by doing whatever we want. We're qualitatively different from someone like you, who brags about counting money under your father's shadow.
Jake's eyes reddened with anger.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: My father is the royal treasury executor. Money solves everything. Amy was the one acting like a spoiled brat.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Is the Carmis family that great? How many of you are actually in power? With my family's influence, we could crush the Carmis family with money. How about it? Want to test that if you don't believe me?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Whew. Thanks to you, I figured it out. Why I accepted the coffee.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: What?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You're different from Shirone. In a word, you're a coward.

blocks.speaker: Jake
blocks.content: Hah! You think I'm more of a coward than that pathetic guy? Want me to prove it right now?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: If you're so eager to do something, hurry up and do it. Don't waste my time blabbering. Do you think I'll be scared just because you're glaring at me, you coward?
Amy smacked Jake's mug.
The expensive coffee splattered against the wall.
The mug shattered into pieces as it hit the stairs.
